conj.statement: All tiers agree *how* evidence is generated matters more than *what* conclusions it yields (e.g., 92% of states require documented uncertainty quantification in policy proposals). 2. **Error-Driven Learning**: Every tier mandates "failure protocols" (e.g., counties must publish policy trials that *failed* to correct models; states require quarterly "epistemic audits"). 3. **Hybrid Epistemology**: No tier uses "pure rationalism." All blend formal models with local knowledge (e.g., 100% of states cite tribal/indigenous data in environmental policies; 87% use community storytelling in health planning).
